description: Adjust the pad to hip height. Lean forward smoothly and return to the starting position.
Place feet on the platform, raise your heels as high as possible, hold, then lower slowly.
Sit comfortably, secure the knee pad, and flex your calves through a full range of motion.
Sit back, keep elbows slightly bent, and bring the handles together in a hugging motion.
Stand between the cables, pull downward and inward to target the lower/inner chest fibers.
Adjust seat so handles are at mid-chest level. Push forward forcefully and return slowly.
Seated press angled downward to target the lower region of the pectoral muscles.
Place feet shoulder-width apart on the platform. Lower your hips until thighs are parallel.
Seated press angled upward to isolate and develop the upper portion of the chest.
Sit under the pads, grip the bar wide, and pull down towards your upper chest while squeezing back.
Alternative wide-grip variation focused on maximizing upper back and lat width.
Sit upright, press your outer arms against the pads, and raise them until parallel to the floor.
Sit back, secure ankles behind pads, and extend legs fully to isolate the quads.
Place feet on the sled, unlock the safety safety locks, lower slowly, and press upward.
Horizontal leg press variation providing stable back support and targeting overall legs.
Hold the cable attachments and elevate your shoulders toward your ears to target upper traps.
Rest arms flat on the pad, grip handles, and curl upwards while keeping elbows fixed.
Sit facing the machine pad, fly arms outward and backward to target posterior deltoids.
Cable variation focused on tension at the peak of the rear delt contraction.
Sit facing outward, grip handles tightly, and push down to isolate the triceps.
Counter-weighted dip station to assist with upper body compound push strength.
Sit tall, pull handles toward your abdomen, keeping elbows tucked close to your body.
Cable row setup offering fluid resistance through the entire rowing motion.
Heavy row variation designed to target mid-to-upper back thickness.
Adjust seat, grip handles, and press straight up to build total shoulder strength.
Lean against the support pad, pull the handles up, squeezing the shoulder blades together.
Slightly altered grip angle version for isolating different segments of the mid-back.
Secure elbows on pad and extend arms downward/forward to isolate the triceps muscle.
Classic cable station attachment using a bar or rope for intensive triceps stimulation.
